contexts.label: NYC Solo Trip
contexts.purpose: Dessa's art show (Tue Apr 28 evening) + Brooklyn exploration

people.name: Dessa
people.note: Art show Tue Apr 28 evening. Busy Wed 2-6pm (Cooper Union class).

people.name: Dean
people.note: Has a clothing store worth visiting.

people.name: Arnold
people.note: Hosting John at 126 Leonard St, Williamsburg.

schedule.notes: Evening: Dessa's art show → drinks after. Visit Dean's store.
2-6pm: Dessa busy (class). Good solo museum day. Evening free.

priorities: Art galleries + exhibitions (Guggenheim Carol Bove, MoMA Marcel Duchamp, Whitney Biennial)
Brooklyn food scene (Williamsburg → Bushwick → Ridgewood corridor)

anchor_experiences.name: MoMA – Marcel Duchamp Retrospective
anchor_experiences.note: First major retrospective in 50+ years. Opens Apr 12.

anchor_experiences.name: The Four Horsemen
anchor_experiences.note: James Murphy's natural wine bar, 295 Grand St. Book Resy ahead.

anchor_experiences.name: Domino Park
anchor_experiences.note: Walking distance from Arnold's. Manhattan skyline views.

anchor_experiences.name: Comedy Cellar
anchor_experiences.note: MONITORING from Apr 13. Standby strategy: arrive hours early.

contexts.label: Boston Long Weekend

travel.note: Flying Porter from Toronto Island (Billy Lake area) — direct and scenic

contexts.purpose: Long weekend escape — good food, history, lively atmosphere, late summer/early fall timing for best energy
contexts.notes: Late Aug or early Sept preferred — shoulder season hotel rates + college energy returning. Freedom Trail walking, North End, harbour. Budget-conscious but nice hotel. GREAT STREETS connection: Commonwealth Avenue (Boston's mall) is profiled in Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets — walk it as a primary activity, not just transit.
mustDo: Walk Commonwealth Avenue (Great Streets by Jacobs)

contexts.label: Great Streets
contexts.notes: Inspired by Allan B. Jacobs' Great Streets (1993). A lifelong project — visiting the streets that define what makes a city feel alive. Jacobs studied ~50 streets worldwide for proportion, edges, trees, uses, detail, and human scale. Each street is a destination, not just a route.

contexts.label: Great Streets — Las Ramblas
contexts.notes: Separate discovery feed for places directly on or immediately off Las Ramblas. Keep this distinct from generic city recommendations. Prioritize places that help understand the street: cafés, bars, bookstores, galleries, institutions, landmark shops, and street-level experiences.
